name: Model Update Alert
description: Notify team to stop using an old model and switch to a new one
body:
🚨 **IMPORTANT MODEL UPDATE — PLEASE READ** 🚨

Team, quick reminder: **do not use {{oldModel}} anymore for {{project}} tasks.**

From now on, please use **{{newModel}}**.

This applies to all new attempts moving forward. Please double-check your model before starting or continuing a task.

Thank you!

name: Generic Urgent Alert
description: General-purpose urgent message for the whole team
body:
🚨 **IMPORTANT UPDATE — PLEASE READ** 🚨

Team, quick reminder: {{message}}

This applies to all new attempts moving forward. Please double-check before starting or continuing a task.

Thank you!

name: Mandatory Webinar Today
description: Announce a mandatory alignment webinar happening today
body:
🚨 **Mandatory alignment webinar — {{date}} at {{timeLabel}}** 🚨

Team, we'll have a mandatory alignment webinar on **{{date}}** at **{{timeLabel}}**.

Webinar link:
{{webinarLink}}

This is for:

{{inviteeList}}

If you were invited, attendance is required. Please join on time so we can align on review/task quality expectations and current project standards.

name: Webinar Starting Now
description: Ping that the webinar is currently live
body:
# Alignment webinar is live now 🚨

Team, we're currently in the reviewer alignment webinar.

Zoom link:
{{webinarLink}}

This is for:

{{inviteeList}}

Please join now if you're listed above 🙏

name: Reminder — 15 Minutes Before
description: 15-minute heads-up before a webinar starts
body:
⏰ Just a reminder — the alignment webinar starts in **15 minutes** at {{timeLabel}}.

Webinar link:
{{webinarLink}}

Please make sure you're ready to join on time 🙏

name: Attendance Required Notice
description: Reminder that attendance is mandatory for invited contributors
body:
Hi team 👋

Just a reminder that attendance is required for the alignment webinar today at **{{timeLabel}}**.

Webinar link:
{{webinarLink}}

Please join on time so we can align on review/task quality expectations and current project standards. Thank you! 🙏

name: Post-Webinar Follow-Up
description: Message after a webinar wraps up
body:
Thanks everyone for joining the alignment webinar! 🙌

Here's a quick recap of what we covered:

{{keyPoints}}

{{recordingLink}}If you missed it, please reach out in the War Room and we'll get you up to speed.

Let's keep the quality bar high — great work everyone 🚀

name: No-Show Follow-Up
description: Follow-up message for contributors who missed the webinar
body:
Hi {{name}}! Just wanted to follow up — we noticed you didn't make it to today's alignment webinar.

Attendance was required for invited contributors. If something came up, please let us know.

War Room link:
{{webinarLink}}

Please make sure to join the next one so you stay aligned with the project expectations 🙏

name: Join War Room for Context
description: Redirect someone to the War Room for a deeper conversation

name: War Room Is Open
description: Let contributors know the War Room is available
body:
Hey team! The War Room is open if you have any questions, blockers, or need support.

War Room link:
{{warRoomLink}}

Feel free to drop in 🙌

name: QM Support Hours
description: Explain when QM support is available in the War Room
body:
Team, quick reminder!

The War Room is usually open throughout the day, but QM support is normally available Monday to Friday, from **10:30 AM to 7:00 PM ARG time**.

War Room link:
{{warRoomLink}}

Time zones:

10:30 AM - 7:00 PM ARG
9:30 AM - 6:00 PM ET / CLT
8:30 AM - 5:00 PM COT / PET
7:30 AM - 4:00 PM Mexico City / Central America
6:30 AM - 3:00 PM PT

Before or after that window, there may not be a QM available, but some experienced contributors from the project may still be around and willing to help with general questions.

For access issues, Cursor setup problems, onboarding/reactivation, or anything that needs QM support, please try to join during the QM coverage window so we can help you faster 🙏

name: Access / Setup Support
description: Direct someone to the War Room for access or Cursor setup help
body:
Hi! For access issues or Cursor setup, the best place to get help is the War Room where we can walk you through it live.

War Room link:
{{warRoomLink}}

Feel free to drop in during QM support hours (Mon–Fri, 10:30 AM–7:00 PM ARG) 🙏

name: Explain Throttle
description: Explain how the throttle system works to a contributor
body:
Hi! The throttle works automatically.

For now, you'll have up to **1 task available every 24 hours** while your initial quality is being reviewed. Once you complete around **3 tasks**, your quality metrics will be evaluated and, based on the results, we can make a decision.

The goal is to make sure the first tasks are aligned with the project standards before enabling more volume.

name: 1 Task Every 24 Hours
description: Short message explaining the 1-task-per-24h limit
body: Hi! Right now you have access to **1 task every 24 hours** while your initial quality is being reviewed. This will be adjusted based on your quality metrics after the first few tasks. Keep up the good work! 🚀

name: Access Adjusted Based on Quality
description: Explain that access was adjusted due to quality metrics
body:
Hi {{name}}! We wanted to let you know that your task access has been adjusted based on your current quality metrics.

Please review the project guidelines and make sure your submissions are aligned with the expected standards before continuing.

If you have questions, feel free to join the War Room and we'll help you get back on track 🙏

name: Added as Reviewer
description: Congratulate a contributor who was added as a reviewer
body:
Hi {{name}}! 👋

You were added as a **reviewer for Stargazer Axiom** based on the quality of your recent work.

Congrats! We really appreciate the work you've been doing 🚀

name: Reviewer Alignment Invite
description: Invite a reviewer to an alignment session
body:
Hi {{name}}! 👋

We'd like to invite you to a reviewer alignment session today at **{{timeLabel}}**.

Zoom link:
{{webinarLink}}

This session is specifically for reviewers and will cover current quality expectations and rubric standards. Attendance is highly recommended 🙏

name: QMA Quality Concern
description: Flag a QMA quality concern to the right person
body:
Hi! Not sure if I'm the right person to ask, and since {{managerName}} is OOO I'm not 100% sure who to reach out to haha, but I wanted to flag this with you.

We've been noticing that this QMA may not be fully aligned with the project quality expectations right now. Some of the tasks have required a lot of follow-up, so we're wondering if there might be another project where he could be a better fit.

Do you know who would be the right person to contact for this type of request?

Thank you!

name: QMA Follow-Up Context
description: Provide context after initial QMA quality concern was raised
body:
Yes, exactly. We did provide follow-up and tried to align him on the project expectations, but even after that, the quality did not improve enough for the current bar.

The main issue is not just one isolated task, but a repeated pattern of tasks needing significant rework/follow-up and not being fully aligned with the project.

Also, for extra context, he was previously an attempter on the project as well, and at that stage he was also struggling to meet the expected quality standards.

From our side, it seems like this may not be the best fit for him right now.

name: Congrats — Course Complete
description: Congratulate a new contributor who completed the course
body:
Hi {{name}}! 👋

Congrats on completing the course! 🎉

Next step: please join the War Room so we can help you request/check your Cursor access and make sure your setup is ready for tasking.

War Room link:
{{warRoomLink}}

Thank you! 🙌

name: Onboarding Tomorrow
description: Invite a contributor to an onboarding session happening tomorrow
body:
Hi {{name}}! 👋

We have an onboarding session scheduled for tomorrow at **{{timeLabel}}**.

Session link:
{{webinarLink}}

This is a great opportunity to get up to speed with the project expectations and ask any questions you have. Hope to see you there! 🙌

name: Mandatory Onboarding
description: Require a contributor to attend a mandatory onboarding session
body:
Hi {{name}}! 👋

You'll need to attend the project onboarding tomorrow at **{{timeLabel}}**.

The goal of the session is to reinforce some important project points and help improve task quality for upcoming submissions.

Please try to be on time. Thank you! 🙏

name: No-Show Onboarding Context
description: Flag contributors who didn't show up to onboarding
body:
Hi {{name}}! Just wanted to give you some context: this morning we set up onboarding sessions for the contributors, but no one joined either session.

My suspicion is that they may not be active or may not be following the project communications, so I wanted to flag it in case it's worth checking their status before we keep coordinating new onboarding sessions.

name: Cursor Access Check
description: Help a contributor check or request their Cursor access
body:
Hi! For Cursor access issues, the best next step is to join the War Room so we can check your access status and help you get set up.

War Room link:
{{warRoomLink}}

Drop in during QM support hours (Mon–Fri, 10:30 AM–7:00 PM ARG) and we'll sort it out 🙏

name: Platform Issue — Task Upload
description: Help a contributor handle a platform issue with a task upload
body:
Hi {{name}}! Thanks for sharing the context and the task ID.

If you can still access the project, task normally, and use Community, it does not sound like you are actually banned. It may be the support bot showing an incorrect status, which we've seen happen before.

For the task issue, please raise the ticket if possible and include the task ID: `{{taskId}}`, plus a short note that `{{fileName}}` was not uploaded due to a platform issue. I'll also flag the context on my side so the team is aware.

Sorry that happened, especially after spending so many hours on it. For now, please continue with your next task if you still have access, and make sure to double-check the uploads before submitting.

name: Model Failure Invalid
description: Explain that a model failure is not a valid task failure
body:
The model failure here does not qualify as a valid task failure. A valid failure means the model attempts the task in good faith and produces a reasonable but incomplete or incorrect solution. In this case, the failure is caused by the task constraints making a correct implementation impossible, not by a model limitation.

Please revise the task to ensure any capable model can attempt it — the failure should come from missing edge cases or quality gaps, not from an impossible prompt.

name: F2P Issue
description: Flag an issue with an F2P test
body:
The F2P test has an issue: it appears to pass in Phase 1 before the gold patch is applied, which means it is not functioning as a true F2P test.

A valid F2P test must fail in Phase 1 (before the fix) and pass in Phase 2 (after the fix). Please review the assertion — it may be too loose, or the test may not be targeting the correct behavior described in the prompt.

name: P2P Issue
description: Flag an issue with a P2P test
body:
The P2P test has an issue: it does not appear to pass reliably in both phases, or it is testing trivial behavior that does not represent a meaningful regression risk.

P2P tests should guard pre-existing functionality that could be accidentally broken by the gold patch. Please revise to ensure the test targets real behavior that is adjacent to the changes made.

name: Rubric Atomicity Fail
description: Flag a rubric criterion that combines multiple checks
body:
This rubric criterion is not atomic because it combines multiple independent requirements into one check. If one part could pass while another fails, the criterion should be split.

Please separate each distinct behavior into its own criterion so QC can assign scores independently.

name: Rubric Self-Containment Fail
description: Flag a rubric criterion that references external context
body:
This rubric criterion is not self-contained. It references external context such as 'existing behavior', 'original implementation', or 'previous state' without providing the concrete expected value.

A grader cannot open the codebase to understand what 'existing' means. Please replace the relative reference with the specific, concrete expected value so the criterion can be evaluated in isolation.

name: Implementation-Coupled Test
description: Flag a test that is coupled to a specific implementation
body:
This test is coupled to a specific implementation detail — it references a method, class, or property name that is not mentioned in the prompt.

A different developer could build a correct solution using different method names, and their solution would fail this test. Please revise to test observable behavior (return values, UI output, API responses) rather than internal implementation.

name: Static Test Issue
description: Flag a test that reads source code instead of executing it
body:
The issue is that this test validates a static implementation detail instead of the actual user-facing behavior. A model could satisfy the test by matching the expected code pattern while still failing to fix the bug described in the prompt.

Tests must execute the code and assert on an observable result. Please remove any use of `fs.readFileSync`, regex on source files, or export name checks.

name: Prompt / Rubric Mismatch
description: Flag a mismatch between the prompt requirements and rubric criteria
body:
There is a mismatch between the prompt requirements and the rubric criteria. Every explicit requirement in the prompt must have a corresponding rubric criterion, and every rubric criterion must trace back to something the prompt asked for.

Please review both documents and ensure full alignment: each requirement maps to a criterion, and each criterion maps to a requirement. Orphan criteria or uncovered requirements should be resolved before submission.

name: Daily Thread Announcement
description: Reusable chat announcement for the daily thread

variables.label: Quick Rule
variables.placeholder: e.g. If your criterion has AND, split it.

body:
Hey team! 👋

Today's [**Stargazer Axiom daily thread**]({{dailyThreadUrl}}) is up.

Please take a few minutes to read it before tasking today. The topic is **{{topic}}**, with a reminder about **{{reminderTitle}}**.

Quick rule: {{quickRule}}

Use the daily thread for blockers, Cursor issues, validation/eval problems, onboarding questions, or task-related doubts 🙌

name: Custom Message
description: Write a fully custom message with your own variables

variables.label: Your Message
variables.placeholder: Write your message here. Use {{variable}} syntax for placeholders.

body: {{message}}
